local_concepts_kp.name: RNN linear interaction distance
local_concepts_kp.description: The recurrent path between two related tokens grows with the number of intervening positions, making long-distance dependencies harder to learn and slowing training.

local_concepts_kp.name: Self-attention as all-pairs contextual lookup
local_concepts_kp.description: Each token can gather information from every token in the sequence in a single parallel operation, rather than relying on stepwise recurrence.

local_concepts_kp.name: Key-query-value self-attention equations
local_concepts_kp.description: Token embeddings are projected into queries, keys, and values; dot-product scores are softmax-normalized and used to compute weighted sums of value vectors.

local_concepts_kp.name: Positional encoding
local_concepts_kp.description: Because self-attention alone ignores order, position information must be injected, commonly by adding sinusoidal or learned positional vectors to token embeddings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Position-wise feed-forward network
local_concepts_kp.description: A per-token MLP applied after attention adds nonlinearity and extra compute so stacked layers do more than repeated weighted averaging.

local_concepts_kp.name: Causal masking
local_concepts_kp.description: Future positions are masked in decoder self-attention so next-token prediction cannot cheat by reading words that come later in the sequence.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multi-head self-attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Instead of one attention operation, transformers run several attention heads in parallel on lower-dimensional projections and then combine their outputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scaled dot-product attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Attention scores are divided by a dimension-based constant so large dot products do not saturate the softmax and kill useful gradients.

local_concepts_kp.name: Residual connections
local_concepts_kp.description: Skip connections add a layer's input to its output, easing optimization by creating direct gradient paths and biasing the network toward identity mappings early in training.

local_concepts_kp.name: Layer normalization
local_concepts_kp.description: Each token vector is normalized across its hidden dimensions so later layers see more stable activations without depending on batch statistics.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transformer architectural variants
local_concepts_kp.description: The same core block yields a decoder-only model with masking, an encoder-only model without masking, and an encoder-decoder model with cross-attention.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transformer scaling advantage and quadratic bottleneck
local_concepts_kp.description: Transformers win through parallelizable large-scale training, but all-pairs attention introduces quadratic sequence-length cost that makes long contexts expensive.

local_concepts_kp.name: Subword tokenization
local_concepts_kp.description: Representing text with learned subword units instead of a fixed whole-word vocabulary so models can cover unseen words, morphology, and typos.

local_concepts_kp.name: Contextual representations
local_concepts_kp.description: Representations whose meaning depends on the surrounding sequence rather than assigning one static vector to each surface form.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pretraining and fine-tuning
local_concepts_kp.description: Two-stage learning in which a model first learns generic language structure from large unlabeled corpora and is then adapted to a supervised downstream task.

local_concepts_kp.name: Language modeling objective
local_concepts_kp.description: Training a model to predict the next token from its prefix, used directly for decoder pretraining and as a baseline pretraining objective more broadly.

local_concepts_kp.name: Masked language modeling
local_concepts_kp.description: Corrupting input text and training an encoder to reconstruct masked tokens using bidirectional context.

local_concepts_kp.name: BERT and encoder transfer
local_concepts_kp.description: Using a pretrained bidirectional encoder such as BERT for downstream tasks by attaching lightweight prediction heads and fine-tuning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning
local_concepts_kp.description: Adapting pretrained models by changing only a small number of parameters, such as prefixes, prompts, or low-rank weight updates.

local_concepts_kp.name: Span corruption for encoder-decoders
local_concepts_kp.description: Pretraining an encoder-decoder by masking contiguous spans in the input and generating the missing spans on the output side.

local_concepts_kp.name: Decoder-only pretraining
local_concepts_kp.description: Training a causal decoder with next-token prediction, the foundation of GPT-style generative models.

local_concepts_kp.name: In-context learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Solving a task from examples placed directly in the prompt, without updating model parameters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scaling and compute–data tradeoffs
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that model quality depends not only on parameter count but also on how many training tokens are used, with compute budget constraining the balance.

local_concepts_kp.name: Chain-of-thought prompting
local_concepts_kp.description: Prompting with worked intermediate reasoning steps so the model generates a scratchpad before the final answer.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hugging Face end-to-end workflow
local_concepts_kp.description: The reusable pattern of selecting a Hub model, loading the corresponding tokenizer and model, preprocessing text, and running inference or fine-tuning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transformer tokenization outputs
local_concepts_kp.description: How raw text becomes input IDs, attention masks, and special-token-augmented sequences aligned with a model's vocabulary.

local_concepts_kp.name: Padding, truncation, and decoding utilities
local_concepts_kp.description: Practical tokenizer controls for batching variable-length text and converting token IDs back into readable strings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Task-specific model classes and architecture-task matching
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that Hugging Face exposes different classes and heads for different tasks, and that valid task choices depend on whether a model is encoder-only, decoder-only, or encoder-decoder.

local_concepts_kp.name: Sequence classification outputs, loss, and predictions
local_concepts_kp.description: How to pass tokenized inputs through a classification model, read logits, obtain loss, and convert logits to predicted labels.

local_concepts_kp.name: Model introspection with hidden states and attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Using Hugging Face model outputs to inspect layerwise hidden states and attention distributions for analysis and debugging.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dataset preparation with datasets and DatasetDict
local_concepts_kp.description: Building train and validation splits, inspecting fields, and preprocessing examples with map so they can feed a transformer model.

local_concepts_kp.name: Manual fine-tuning with PyTorch
local_concepts_kp.description: Treating Hugging Face models as standard PyTorch modules inside a DataLoader-based training loop with optimizer and scheduler.

local_concepts_kp.name: Trainer API, metrics, callbacks, and checkpoints
local_concepts_kp.description: The higher-level Hugging Face training abstraction that packages configuration, metrics, evaluation, callbacks, and model persistence.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pretraining scale and emergent capabilities
local_concepts_kp.description: Large-scale next-token pretraining creates broad capabilities that post-training later directs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Zero-shot and few-shot in-context learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Task behavior induced by prompts and examples without parameter updates.

local_concepts_kp.name: Chain-of-thought prompting
local_concepts_kp.description: Prompting with or inducing intermediate reasoning steps for multi-step tasks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Instruction fine-tuning
local_concepts_kp.description: Supervised training on instruction-output pairs so models follow user intent.

local_concepts_kp.name: LLM evaluation and instruction-data scaling
local_concepts_kp.description: Benchmarks such as MMLU, benchmark caveats, and scaling effects in instruction tuning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Limitations of supervised fine-tuning
local_concepts_kp.description: SFT limitations including expensive demonstrations, open-ended outputs, token-loss mismatch, and human quality bottlenecks.

local_concepts_kp.name: RLHF preference optimization
local_concepts_kp.description: Optimizing language models for learned human preference rewards over model-generated outputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Preference comparisons and Bradley-Terry modeling
local_concepts_kp.description: Turning pairwise human preferences into a reward-model objective based on reward differences.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reward hacking and KL regularization
local_concepts_kp.description: The risk of exploiting learned rewards and the KL-style penalty that keeps the policy near the reference model.

local_concepts_kp.name: Direct Preference Optimization
local_concepts_kp.description: A direct classification-style preference objective that avoids a separate RLHF optimization loop.

local_concepts_kp.name: InstructGPT and ChatGPT post-training pipeline
local_concepts_kp.description: The practical assistant-building pipeline and its remaining preference-alignment risks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Natural language generation
local_concepts_kp.description: The family of NLP tasks whose output is natural language intended to be fluent, coherent, and useful for people.

local_concepts_kp.name: Open-endedness and entropy in NLG tasks
local_concepts_kp.description: A way to categorize generation tasks by how many valid outputs they permit, with entropy used as a formal intuition for openness.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autoregressive language modeling
local_concepts_kp.description: Left-to-right generation where each next token is predicted from previously generated context and fed back into the model.

local_concepts_kp.name: Teacher forcing and student forcing
local_concepts_kp.description: The contrast between training with gold previous tokens and generating with the model's own previous outputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Degeneration under maximum-probability decoding
local_concepts_kp.description: The failure mode where greedy or beam-style search produces repetitive, overconfident text in open-ended generation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Sampling-based decoding controls
local_concepts_kp.description: The family of stochastic decoding methods and hyperparameters such as top-k, top-p, epsilon, typical sampling, and temperature.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reranking sampled candidates
local_concepts_kp.description: Selecting among multiple sampled generations using auxiliary quality scores such as style, coherence, or factuality.

local_concepts_kp.name: Exposure bias
local_concepts_kp.description: The train-test mismatch caused by training on gold prefixes but generating on self-produced prefixes.

local_concepts_kp.name: Training alternatives to reduce exposure bias
local_concepts_kp.description: Methods such as scheduled sampling, DAgger, and retrieval-augmented generation that make training conditions closer to generation conditions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reinforcement learning and RLHF for NLG
local_concepts_kp.description: Training text generators with reward functions, including human-preference reward models layered on top of pretrained systems.

local_concepts_kp.name: Automatic evaluation metrics for NLG
local_concepts_kp.description: The landscape of reference-based lexical and embedding-based automatic metrics used to score generated text.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human-centered evaluation and metric alignment
local_concepts_kp.description: Using human judgments as the gold standard and measuring whether automatic metrics agree with those judgments.

local_concepts_kp.name: Ethical risks and deployment safeguards for NLG
local_concepts_kp.description: The safety, bias, truthfulness, and adversarial robustness considerations that should gate deployment of text generators.

local_concepts_kp.name: Evaluation objectives by lifecycle stage
local_concepts_kp.description: Different phases such as training, development, deployment, and publishing need different evaluation properties.

local_concepts_kp.name: Academic benchmark design tradeoffs
local_concepts_kp.description: Academic benchmarks prioritize reproducibility, standardization, ease of use, and directional progress over perfect realism.

local_concepts_kp.name: Close-ended NLP evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Tasks with a small answer space can often be evaluated with standard classification metrics.

local_concepts_kp.name: Metric choice and aggregation pitfalls
local_concepts_kp.description: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and aggregate leaderboard averages can mislead when class balance or metric semantics differ.

local_concepts_kp.name: Spurious correlations from dataset construction
local_concepts_kp.description: Benchmarks can contain shortcuts that let models exploit artifacts rather than solve the intended task.

local_concepts_kp.name: Open-ended generation evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Open-ended tasks have many valid answers and graded quality, so evaluation must move beyond exact labels.

local_concepts_kp.name: BLEU and ROUGE overlap metrics
local_concepts_kp.description: Reference-based overlap metrics compare generated text to human references using lexical or n-gram overlap.

local_concepts_kp.name: BERTScore and BLEURT
local_concepts_kp.description: Embedding-based and learned evaluation metrics use contextual representations or supervised fine-tuning to improve over lexical overlap.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reference quality limits reference-based evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Even a decent scoring metric can fail if the human references used as gold outputs are weak or noisy.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human evaluation design and disagreement
local_concepts_kp.description: Human evaluation remains the gold standard but suffers from cost, slowness, disagreement, and protocol sensitivity.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pairwise chatbot evaluation and Elo ranking
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern chat systems are commonly evaluated by side-by-side preferences aggregated into rankings such as Elo scores.

local_concepts_kp.name: LLM-as-a-judge and AlpacaEval
local_concepts_kp.description: Judge models such as GPT-4 can replace or augment humans for fast pairwise evaluation, but introduce systematic biases.

local_concepts_kp.name: Current LLM benchmark suites and task coverage
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern LLM evaluation uses broad benchmark suites spanning math, knowledge, code, law, medicine, and other domains.

local_concepts_kp.name: Perplexity as a proxy metric
local_concepts_kp.description: Pretraining or validation perplexity often tracks downstream performance but is not universally comparable or sufficient.

local_concepts_kp.name: Consistency, contamination, and overfitting in benchmarks
local_concepts_kp.description: Benchmark scores can shift because of formatting, implementation differences, train-test leakage, or repeated tuning on public tests.

local_concepts_kp.name: Monoculture, multilinguality, fairness, efficiency, and incentives
local_concepts_kp.description: Benchmarking choices are shaped by field-wide incentives, often producing English-centric, accuracy-only evaluations that miss important deployment concerns.

local_concepts_kp.name: Floating-point range vs precision
local_concepts_kp.description: How FP32, FP16, and related formats allocate bits to sign, exponent/range, and mantissa/precision, and why that affects underflow, overflow, and memory cost.

local_concepts_kp.name: Mixed precision training
local_concepts_kp.description: Training scheme that uses low-precision compute for speed and memory savings while retaining higher-precision state for stable updates.

local_concepts_kp.name: Loss scaling for FP16 training
local_concepts_kp.description: Technique that multiplies the loss before backpropagation so FP16 gradients are less likely to underflow, then unscales before the optimizer step.

local_concepts_kp.name: BFloat16
local_concepts_kp.description: Half-precision format with FP32-like exponent range and lower mantissa precision, often preferred for mixed-precision training on supported hardware.

local_concepts_kp.name: Distributed Data Parallel (DDP)
local_concepts_kp.description: Multi-GPU training setup where each worker holds a full replica, processes a data shard, and synchronizes gradients across workers.

local_concepts_kp.name: Collective communication primitives
local_concepts_kp.description: All-reduce, reduce-scatter, all-gather, and reduce as the core synchronization operations behind DDP, ZeRO, and FSDP.

local_concepts_kp.name: ZeRO optimizer and gradient sharding
local_concepts_kp.description: ZeRO stages 1 and 2 reduce memory by sharding optimizer state and then gradients across workers while preserving synchronized updates.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP / ZeRO stage 3)
local_concepts_kp.description: Stage-3 sharding scheme that partitions parameters, gradients, and optimizer state, then gathers per-layer weights on demand during forward and backward passes.

local_concepts_kp.name: Activation memory as a separate bottleneck
local_concepts_kp.description: Model activations must be stored for backpropagation, scale with batch size, and are not automatically reduced by parameter or optimizer sharding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT)
local_concepts_kp.description: Fine-tuning approach that updates only a small subset of model parameters to reduce memory, storage, and sometimes overfitting risk.

local_concepts_kp.name: LoRA low-rank adaptation
local_concepts_kp.description: PEFT method that freezes base weights and learns a low-rank update for selected matrices, usually controlled by rank and scaling hyperparameters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Practical fine-tuning decision flowchart
local_concepts_kp.description: Operational decision rule for choosing mixed precision, ZeRO stage 2 or 3, checkpointing, and LoRA based on hardware and memory constraints.

local_concepts_kp.name: FSDP sharding policy and communication overlap
local_concepts_kp.description: Implementation-level idea that FSDP efficiency depends on how layers are grouped into units and how communication is overlapped with computation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Text CNN framing
local_concepts_kp.description: CNNs model language with local receptive fields over token sequences instead of sequential state updates.

local_concepts_kp.name: 1D convolution over n-grams
local_concepts_kp.description: A text convolution slides a learned filter over adjacent word-vector windows to compute local features.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pooling strategies in text CNNs
local_concepts_kp.description: Max, average, local, and k-max pooling determine how local activations are summarized across a sentence.

local_concepts_kp.name: Padding, stride, and dilation
local_concepts_kp.description: These hyperparameters control output length, overlap, and receptive field in convolutional text models.

local_concepts_kp.name: Kim 2014 sentence-classification CNN
local_concepts_kp.description: A multi-filter text CNN with max pooling and softmax classification that became the canonical early NLP CNN baseline.

local_concepts_kp.name: Frozen plus fine-tuned embedding channels
local_concepts_kp.description: Using both a static and an adaptive embedding channel mitigates harmful drift when fine-tuning on small labeled data.

local_concepts_kp.name: VD-CNN character-level text classification
local_concepts_kp.description: A very deep residual CNN starting from characters can perform competitive text classification without pretrained word vectors.

local_concepts_kp.name: Batch normalization and 1x1 convolutions
local_concepts_kp.description: These CNN building blocks stabilize training and create position-wise feature transformations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Compositional tree semantics
local_concepts_kp.description: Phrase meanings can be built compositionally over parse trees rather than only over flat token sequences.

local_concepts_kp.name: Greedy tree recursive parser
local_concepts_kp.description: A recursive network can score and merge adjacent constituents greedily to build a binary parse and sentence representation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Recursive neural tensor network
local_concepts_kp.description: An RNTN uses tensor-mediated interactions to model richer phrase composition than a standard recursive neural network.

local_concepts_kp.name: Phrase-level sentiment supervision
local_concepts_kp.description: Labeling every phrase node in a parse tree creates richer supervision than sentence-level labels alone.

local_concepts_kp.name: Negation and compositional sentiment
local_concepts_kp.description: Modeling how modifiers and negation change sentiment requires structure-sensitive composition, not just keyword counts.

local_concepts_kp.name: Tree models versus transformers
local_concepts_kp.description: Tree recursive models may match linguistic structure better in some cases, but transformers win on flexible information flow and overall power.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodality: definition, motivation, and task framing
local_concepts_kp.description: The lecture’s framing concept that multimodality means learning from multiple information types and is motivated by human perception, internet data, and scaling needs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Cross-modal alignment and grounding
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that language and perception can be aligned in shared spaces or through predictive grounding objectives to improve semantic representations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodal feature extraction
local_concepts_kp.description: How raw images or other signals are turned into vectors, including region features, ConvNet features, and ViT patch embeddings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodal fusion strategies
local_concepts_kp.description: The choice of when and how modalities interact, including early, middle, and late fusion, plus modulation and attention-based schemes.

local_concepts_kp.name: Contrastive vision-language pretraining
local_concepts_kp.description: The CLIP/ALIGN family of models that align independently encoded images and text with contrastive objectives and scale primarily through data and compute.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodal transformer pretraining
local_concepts_kp.description: Single-stream and dual-stream transformer architectures that combine image and text tokens, often with masked prediction and image-text matching objectives.

local_concepts_kp.name: Unified multimodal foundation models
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that one jointly pretrained model should handle unimodal and multimodal tasks rather than requiring separate systems.

local_concepts_kp.name: Generative multimodal language models
local_concepts_kp.description: Decoder-centric multimodal systems that condition large language models on visual inputs through projections, cross-attention, or adapter-like components.

local_concepts_kp.name: Evaluation and benchmark design
local_concepts_kp.description: The need to build tests that force multimodal reasoning and expose shortcut behavior, compositional failures, and distributional bias.

local_concepts_kp.name: Beyond images: audio, video, embodied learning, and new modalities
local_concepts_kp.description: Generalizing the multimodal recipe to speech, audio, video, interaction, 3D generation, and even olfactory grounding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Clinical motivation for speech BCI
local_concepts_kp.description: Speech BCIs are motivated by patients who retain cognition but lose the ability to speak or move, making direct neural communication restoration valuable.

local_concepts_kp.name: External versus invasive brain-signal recording
local_concepts_kp.description: BCI performance depends strongly on where signals are recorded: scalp EEG is safer but coarse, while intracortical recordings are more precise.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural spikes as the basic recorded unit
local_concepts_kp.description: Single-neuron recordings are framed in terms of action potentials and spike trains measured near neurons.

local_concepts_kp.name: Population decoding from noisy motor-cortex activity
local_concepts_kp.description: Movement intent is inferred by combining noisy single-neuron tuning information across multiple channels and learning decision boundaries.

local_concepts_kp.name: Recording modalities and microelectrode arrays
local_concepts_kp.description: Different BCI sensing technologies trade spatial and temporal resolution, with microelectrode arrays offering fine-grained intracortical access.

local_concepts_kp.name: Movement-based BCI applications
local_concepts_kp.description: Intracortical decoders can restore interaction through cursor control, robotic-arm control, and handwriting-based communication.

local_concepts_kp.name: Speech-motor decoding through phonemes
local_concepts_kp.description: For speech neuroprostheses, the lecture proposes targeting oral-facial motor cortex and decoding discrete phonemes instead of continuous articulator trajectories.

local_concepts_kp.name: T12 speech-neuroprosthesis findings
local_concepts_kp.description: The T12 case study shows where useful speech information appeared in the brain and demonstrates real-time attempted-speech and silent-speech decoding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Paired neural-text data collection
local_concepts_kp.description: Building a speech decoder requires supervised paired recordings of neural activity and target utterances collected under clinical constraints.

local_concepts_kp.name: CTC for monotonic neural-to-phoneme alignment
local_concepts_kp.description: Connectionist Temporal Classification solves the mismatch between long neural sequences and shorter phoneme outputs when alignment is monotonic but unknown.

local_concepts_kp.name: Real-time decoding stack with GRUs, beam search, and language models
local_concepts_kp.description: The final system couples a GRU acoustic-style decoder with beam search, lexicon constraints, fast n-gram language modeling, and transformer reranking.

local_concepts_kp.name: Future BCIs, inner speech, and neuroethics
local_concepts_kp.description: Future BCI systems may become multimodal and less effortful, but they also raise privacy, consent, memory, and enhancement questions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Deep learning for NLP course framing
local_concepts_kp.description: The course is positioned as a bottom-up introduction to neural NLP methods that also aims to teach practical system building and linguistic understanding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Language as communication and cognitive scaffolding
local_concepts_kp.description: Language is presented as both a communication system and a scaffold for higher-level human thought, planning, and cultural transmission.

local_concepts_kp.name: Modern neural NLP and foundation models
local_concepts_kp.description: Recent neural methods enable machine translation, answer engines, large language models, and multimodal foundation models.

local_concepts_kp.name: Denotational semantics
local_concepts_kp.description: A classical view of meaning in which symbols are paired with their denotations or referents.

local_concepts_kp.name: Distributional semantics
local_concepts_kp.description: A word’s meaning is inferred from the contexts in which it appears, summarized by the slogan that a word is known by the company it keeps.

local_concepts_kp.name: Limitations of one-hot localist word representations
local_concepts_kp.description: One-hot vectors identify words but do not encode graded similarity, since different words are orthogonal under dot product.

local_concepts_kp.name: Word vectors and embedding spaces
local_concepts_kp.description: Words are represented as dense vectors in a high-dimensional space where semantic relationships correspond to geometric structure such as large dot products and clustered neighborhoods.

local_concepts_kp.name: Static embeddings versus contextual meaning
local_concepts_kp.description: A single embedding per word string cannot fully disambiguate sense by context, though it can average across senses in a useful way.

local_concepts_kp.name: Word2vec objective: predict nearby context words
local_concepts_kp.description: Word2vec learns embeddings by maximizing the probability of observed context words around a center word across a corpus.

local_concepts_kp.name: Softmax probability model for context prediction
local_concepts_kp.description: The conditional probability of an outside word given a center word is defined by exponentiating dot products and normalizing over the vocabulary.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient-based learning and observed-minus-expected updates
local_concepts_kp.description: The word2vec parameters are optimized by differentiating the log-softmax objective, yielding gradients that compare observed context vectors against the model’s expected prediction.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reasoning taxonomy and informal deductive reasoning
local_concepts_kp.description: The lecture distinguishes deductive, inductive, and abductive reasoning and then narrows its focus to informal deductive multi-step reasoning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Chain-of-thought prompting
local_concepts_kp.description: A prompting strategy that asks the model to generate intermediate reasoning before answering, either via exemplars or zero-shot prompting.

local_concepts_kp.name: Self-consistency decoding
local_concepts_kp.description: Sample multiple rationales and answers, then choose the most frequent answer as a more reliable prediction.

local_concepts_kp.name: Least-to-most prompting
local_concepts_kp.description: A decomposition strategy where the model first breaks a problem into sub-problems, solves them, and then composes the final answer.

local_concepts_kp.name: Distilling reasoning traces into smaller models
local_concepts_kp.description: Use a strong teacher model to generate step-by-step explanations and fine-tune a smaller model on those explanations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Iterative self-training on rationales
local_concepts_kp.description: A model improves by generating many rationales, filtering for correct ones, fine-tuning on them, and repeating the cycle.

local_concepts_kp.name: Faithfulness and counterfactual evaluation of reasoning
local_concepts_kp.description: Reasoning quality should be tested by whether rationales causally affect answers and whether performance survives distribution shifts.

local_concepts_kp.name: Language-conditioned agents
local_concepts_kp.description: An agent receives observations from an environment and a language instruction, then selects actions to complete the instructed task.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pre-LLM instruction-following paradigms
local_concepts_kp.description: Historical approaches include semantic parsing into logical forms, executable plans, and reinforcement learning policies.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autoregressive trajectory modeling and prompting in a loop
local_concepts_kp.description: Model action selection as next-token prediction over state-action histories and repeatedly query the model for the next action.

local_concepts_kp.name: Agent benchmarks and evaluation environments
local_concepts_kp.description: MiniWoB, WebArena, and WebLINX provide progressively more realistic settings for measuring task success in browser-like environments.

local_concepts_kp.name: Synthetic demonstrations through exploration and relabeling
local_concepts_kp.description: Generate agent training data by exploring environments, labeling trajectories with inferred instructions, and iteratively refining both.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodal UI agents
local_concepts_kp.description: Use vision-language models such as LLaVA and Pix2Struct so agents can act from screenshots or pixels rather than only text or DOM.

local_concepts_kp.name: Current limitations and human-model gap
local_concepts_kp.description: Despite progress, current reasoning and agent systems remain brittle, prompt-sensitive, and far below human reliability on longer tasks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Post-training and RLHF importance
local_concepts_kp.description: RLHF/post-training as a central stage in making pretrained LMs useful chat models.

local_concepts_kp.name: Instruction tuning and alignment vocabulary
local_concepts_kp.description: Definitions of instruction fine-tuning, supervised fine-tuning, alignment, and preference fine-tuning.

local_concepts_kp.name: RLHF objective and preference rewards
local_concepts_kp.description: The RLHF reward-maximization objective with KL regularization and Bradley-Terry-style pairwise rewards.

local_concepts_kp.name: Direct Preference Optimization
local_concepts_kp.description: DPO as a simpler preference-optimization method that avoids full PPO-style RL infrastructure.

local_concepts_kp.name: Open alignment data and adoption
local_concepts_kp.description: ShareGPT, Open Assistant, UltraFeedback, Zephyr, and Tulu 2 as drivers of open alignment progress.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reward modeling and RewardBench
local_concepts_kp.description: Pairwise reward-model training and RewardBench as local evaluation for reward models.

local_concepts_kp.name: Safety/refusal evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Benchmarking whether models refuse harmful requests, over-refuse, or answer everything.

local_concepts_kp.name: DPO implicit reward/reference caveat
local_concepts_kp.description: DPO reward depends on policy/reference log-probability ratios, so missing reference checkpoints break reward reuse.

local_concepts_kp.name: DPO versus PPO and online methods
local_concepts_kp.description: The empirical and conceptual tradeoff between simple DPO, more costly PPO, and online DPO variants.

local_concepts_kp.name: Industry recipes and future directions
local_concepts_kp.description: Iterative SFT/rejection-sampling/DPO/PPO pipelines plus data, scale, evaluation, and personalization directions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Preference formats beyond pairwise
local_concepts_kp.description: One-sided, K-wise, learning-to-rank, and fine-grained preference labels.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reward hacking
local_concepts_kp.description: Optimizers exploiting incomplete reward specifications in reward-model-based alignment.

local_concepts_kp.name: Distributional semantics
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that linguistic representations gain meaning from the contexts in which words occur; presented as a central engine of modern NLP.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scaling, pretraining, and foundation models
local_concepts_kp.description: The modern recipe of language-model pretraining, post-training, and scaling compute/data/model size to obtain strong general-purpose NLP systems.

local_concepts_kp.name: Generalization versus memorization
local_concepts_kp.description: The concern that LLM success may rely heavily on memorized exposure rather than flexible generalization from limited data.

local_concepts_kp.name: Mechanistic interpretability
local_concepts_kp.description: Research that tries to explain what internal neural-network computations and components are doing.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multilingual and low-resource NLP gaps
local_concepts_kp.description: The mismatch between strong English-centric performance and much weaker support for many other languages, especially low-resource ones.

local_concepts_kp.name: Benchmark contamination and evaluation integrity
local_concepts_kp.description: The problem that benchmark examples may leak into pretraining data, undermining claims of true generalization.

local_concepts_kp.name: Domain-specific and socially situated NLP
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that NLP behavior changes across legal, medical, and social contexts, where failures can cause real harm.

local_concepts_kp.name: The capability–limitation duality of LLMs
local_concepts_kp.description: The view that GPT-4-class systems are simultaneously astonishingly useful and still qualitatively limited.

local_concepts_kp.name: Symbolic AI versus cybernetics
local_concepts_kp.description: The historical divide between logic-based symbolic AI and the neural/cybernetic tradition.

local_concepts_kp.name: Language as a symbolic communication system processed by neural brains
local_concepts_kp.description: The claim that language is symbolic, but this does not require symbolic internal processors in brains or machines.

local_concepts_kp.name: Linguistics and language as scaffolding for thought
local_concepts_kp.description: The view that linguistic theory supplies useful distinctions for AI and that language supports higher-order human cognition.

local_concepts_kp.name: Model-theoretic semantics and semantic parsing
local_concepts_kp.description: The classical denotational pipeline that maps sentences to formal meanings and executable representations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Use theory and gradient grounding of meaning
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that meaning can be partial, graded, and grounded through use, context, and multiple kinds of connection rather than only denotation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Present-day AI harms, power concentration, and disinformation
local_concepts_kp.description: A risk framework that prioritizes immediate social harms and power asymmetries over speculative extinction scenarios.

local_concepts_kp.name: Shared representational overlap
local_concepts_kp.description: Humans and machines need partially overlapping representations or vocabulary to exchange meaning and collaborate effectively.

local_concepts_kp.name: Move 37 as a superhuman concept exemplar
local_concepts_kp.description: AlphaGo’s Move 37 is used as a concrete case of a machine-discovered strategy that humans could recognize as important without yet being able to explain it.

local_concepts_kp.name: Limits of attribution methods for behavioral inference
local_concepts_kp.description: Saliency, IG, and SHAP may look plausible but often do not justify conclusions about what a model actually uses or how it will behave.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hypothesis-testing view of local explanation and sampling
local_concepts_kp.description: Interpreting attribution can be cast as hypothesis testing, and direct local sampling offers a more reliable way to estimate function shape.

local_concepts_kp.name: Localization-editing mismatch in LLMs
local_concepts_kp.description: Where a fact appears to be localized in a transformer is not the same as where editing that fact will work best.

local_concepts_kp.name: Causal tracing as forward-pass localization
local_concepts_kp.description: Causal tracing can still reveal where factual information is active during computation even if that signal does not predict editability.

local_concepts_kp.name: Observational discovery of emergent RL behaviors
local_concepts_kp.description: Latent-variable modeling of state-action traces can reveal recurring multi-agent behaviors without hand labeling every trajectory.

local_concepts_kp.name: Concept bottlenecks for controllable RL interventions
local_concepts_kp.description: Embedding explicit concepts inside agents enables targeted intervention studies that expose coordination structure and causal dependencies.

local_concepts_kp.name: Inter-agent relationship graphs from interventions
local_concepts_kp.description: Graphs built from intervention outcomes can reveal hidden social or coordination structure that raw statistics miss.

local_concepts_kp.name: Teaching humans superhuman machine concepts
local_concepts_kp.description: A long-term goal is to discover strategies or concepts from models like AlphaZero that humans do not know and test whether people can learn them.

local_concepts_kp.name: Cross-domain transfer and human-language interfaces
local_concepts_kp.description: Interpretability methods mix domain-general theory with domain-specific deployment, and human-language interfaces help but cannot fully capture machine-native concepts.

local_concepts_kp.name: Python for NLP and deep learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Why Python is used in CS224N-style work: readability, scientific computation, and integration with deep learning frameworks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dynamic variables and basic Python operations
local_concepts_kp.description: Variable assignment, dynamic typing, arithmetic, exponentiation, and type casting in Python.

local_concepts_kp.name: Python truth values, None, operators, and indentation
local_concepts_kp.description: True/False capitalization, None, logical operators, equality versus assignment, and consistent indentation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Colab and notebook workflow
local_concepts_kp.description: Interactive notebook execution for running code cell by cell and debugging with intermediate outputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Python lists, slicing, copying, and negative indexing
local_concepts_kp.description: Mutable lists, zero-indexing, append, len, concatenation, slicing semantics, copy behavior, and negative indexing.

local_concepts_kp.name: Tuples and immutability
local_concepts_kp.description: Tuple creation, indexing, immutability, and single-element tuple notation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dictionaries for fast key-value mapping
local_concepts_kp.description: Dictionary/hash-map behavior, membership checks, deletion, and mapping strings/items to numeric indices.

local_concepts_kp.name: Python loops and iteration idioms
local_concepts_kp.description: range, direct iteration over elements, enumerate, and dictionary key/value/item iteration.

local_concepts_kp.name: NumPy as an optimized numerical library
local_concepts_kp.description: NumPy's role in efficient mathematical, matrix, and vector operations through optimized lower-level routines.

local_concepts_kp.name: Matrices, vectors, tensors, and NumPy array shapes
local_concepts_kp.description: Core shape vocabulary for rectangular matrices, one-dimensional vectors, higher-dimensional tensors, and NumPy shape notation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reshape and dimensional representation
local_concepts_kp.description: How (3,), (1,3), and (3,1) differ, and how reshape changes array dimensions when element counts match.

local_concepts_kp.name: Axis-based reductions and keepdims
local_concepts_kp.description: Using axis arguments to reduce over dimensions and keepdims to preserve dimensionality.

local_concepts_kp.name: Elementwise multiplication, matrix multiplication, and dot products
local_concepts_kp.description: Distinguishing *, np.matmul/@, and np.dot behavior with attention to dimensional compatibility.

local_concepts_kp.name: NumPy indexing, Boolean masks, and new axes
local_concepts_kp.description: Colon indexing, row/column selection, Boolean indexing, and adding singleton dimensions with newaxis-like syntax.

local_concepts_kp.name: NumPy broadcasting rules
local_concepts_kp.description: Compatibility rules for operating on arrays of different shapes by implicitly duplicating singleton dimensions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Vectorization instead of Python loops
local_concepts_kp.description: Using NumPy indexing and array operations instead of slow Python loops over large matrices.

local_concepts_kp.name: PyTorch tensors
local_concepts_kp.description: PyTorch tensors are multidimensional arrays analogous to NumPy arrays and are the core data structure for representing inputs, intermediate states, and model parameters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Tensor shapes and broadcasting
local_concepts_kp.description: Reasoning about tensor dimensions, shape compatibility, and broadcasting is necessary for elementwise operations, batching, and matrix multiplication.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reshaping, reduction, and indexing
local_concepts_kp.description: PyTorch supports reshaping tensors, reducing dimensions with operations like sum and mean, and NumPy-style slicing and indexing for selecting substructures.

local_concepts_kp.name: Shape-based debugging
local_concepts_kp.description: Printing and checking tensor shapes is a core debugging habit for understanding errors and verifying that operations behave as intended.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autograd
local_concepts_kp.description: Autograd tracks tensor computations so gradients can be computed automatically during the backward pass without manual derivative code.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient accumulation and zero_grad
local_concepts_kp.description: PyTorch accumulates gradients across backward passes, so training code must explicitly zero them before each new optimization step.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural-network modules and composition
local_concepts_kp.description: PyTorch provides reusable modules such as linear layers, activations, and Sequential blocks, and custom networks are created by subclassing nn.Module.

local_concepts_kp.name: PyTorch training loop
local_concepts_kp.description: A standard PyTorch training loop combines forward computation, loss evaluation, backward gradient computation, and an optimizer step inside an epoch loop.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient descent and learning rate
local_concepts_kp.description: Gradient-based optimization updates parameters by subtracting a scaled gradient, with the learning rate controlling step size.

local_concepts_kp.name: Stochastic gradient descent and mini-batches
local_concepts_kp.description: SGD estimates gradients from small data subsets, trading exactness for speed and useful optimization noise.

local_concepts_kp.name: Random initialization and symmetry breaking
local_concepts_kp.description: Word-vector parameters must start as small random values rather than all zeros to avoid false symmetries.

local_concepts_kp.name: Word2vec skip-gram bag-of-words objective
local_concepts_kp.description: The skip-gram model predicts context words from a center word using dot products between center and outside vectors.

local_concepts_kp.name: Word vector nearest-neighbor similarity
local_concepts_kp.description: Semantic similarity can be inspected by asking which vocabulary vectors are nearest to a query word.

local_concepts_kp.name: Linear semantic components and analogies
local_concepts_kp.description: Vector differences can encode relations, enabling analogies such as King - man + woman ≈ Queen.

local_concepts_kp.name: Two-vector word2vec parameterization
local_concepts_kp.description: Word2vec usually learns separate center and outside vectors and averages or otherwise combines them after training.

local_concepts_kp.name: Naive softmax vs word2vec model variants
local_concepts_kp.description: Word2vec includes skip-gram and CBOW structures, and naive softmax computes a full vocabulary normalization.

local_concepts_kp.name: Negative sampling with sigmoid objectives
local_concepts_kp.description: Negative sampling replaces full softmax with logistic decisions that distinguish true context words from sampled noise words.

local_concepts_kp.name: Unigram distribution to the 3/4 power
local_concepts_kp.description: The negative-sampling distribution uses unigram probabilities raised to 3/4 to upweight less frequent words relative to raw frequency.

local_concepts_kp.name: Co-occurrence matrix word representations
local_concepts_kp.description: Count-based embeddings start by counting how often words occur in each other's context windows.

local_concepts_kp.name: SVD dimensionality reduction for word vectors
local_concepts_kp.description: SVD can reduce a large co-occurrence matrix into lower-dimensional word representations by truncating smaller singular values.

local_concepts_kp.name: GloVe log-bilinear co-occurrence-ratio model
local_concepts_kp.description: GloVe uses log co-occurrence ratios so vector differences correspond to meaning differences.

local_concepts_kp.name: Intrinsic versus extrinsic evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Intrinsic evaluation tests internal subtasks, while extrinsic evaluation measures effects on real downstream systems.

local_concepts_kp.name: Word-vector analogy and similarity benchmarks
local_concepts_kp.description: Embedding quality can be measured by analogy accuracy or correlation with human word-similarity judgments.

local_concepts_kp.name: Named entity recognition as an extrinsic embedding task
local_concepts_kp.description: NER labels spans as people, places, and other entities, and can test whether word vectors improve a downstream NLP system.

local_concepts_kp.name: Polysemy and multi-sense embeddings
local_concepts_kp.description: Words often have multiple related or unrelated meanings, and one approach clusters token occurrences into sense-specific embeddings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Single-vector averages and sparse coding for senses
local_concepts_kp.description: A single word vector can average contexts, while sparse coding can sometimes recover sense vectors from high-dimensional sparse embeddings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Supervised window classification
local_concepts_kp.description: A window classifier predicts a label for a target word using its surrounding context and word-vector features.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural classifiers as learned nonlinear representations
local_concepts_kp.description: Neural classifiers learn both weights and distributed representations, enabling nonlinear classification in the original input space.

local_concepts_kp.name: Feed-forward neural network layer
local_concepts_kp.description: A neural layer concatenates embeddings, applies an affine transformation plus nonlinearity, and maps hidden features to output scores.

local_concepts_kp.name: Cross-entropy loss and negative log likelihood
local_concepts_kp.description: For one-hot targets, cross entropy reduces to the model's log probability of the correct class and is optimized as negative log likelihood.

local_concepts_kp.name: Artificial neuron as logistic regression
local_concepts_kp.description: A simplified neuron combines weighted inputs, applies a nonlinearity, and with logistic activation is equivalent to logistic regression.

local_concepts_kp.name: Depth and hidden feature learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Multi-layer neural networks stack many simple units so intermediate layers learn useful representations automatically.

local_concepts_kp.name: Intermediate representation learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Multilayer neural networks are powerful because hidden layers can learn useful internal features for downstream prediction instead of relying only on manually defined inputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Activation nonlinearities
local_concepts_kp.description: Differentiable nonlinear activation functions such as sigmoid, tanh, ReLU, and ReLU-like variants are needed so networks can model nonlinear functions and support gradient-based learning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient-based learning and SGD
local_concepts_kp.description: Neural networks are trained by computing the gradient of an objective and updating parameters with stochastic gradient descent.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient and Jacobian in vectorized calculus
local_concepts_kp.description: For multivariable functions, scalar outputs use gradient vectors and multi-output functions use Jacobian matrices of partial derivatives.

local_concepts_kp.name: Chain rule for composed functions
local_concepts_kp.description: Gradients of composed scalar or vector functions are computed by multiplying derivatives or Jacobians along the computation path.

local_concepts_kp.name: Layer-wise Jacobians for activations and affine maps
local_concepts_kp.description: Elementwise activations produce diagonal Jacobians, while affine maps such as Wx+b yield simple derivatives with respect to inputs and biases.

local_concepts_kp.name: Upstream gradient reuse and outer-product parameter gradients
local_concepts_kp.description: Shared upstream error signals should be reused across parameter derivatives, and weight gradients often combine the upstream signal with the local input in an outer-product form.

local_concepts_kp.name: Shape convention for gradients
local_concepts_kp.description: In practice, gradients are stored in the same tensor shapes as the parameters they update, even when raw Jacobian form would have a different layout.

local_concepts_kp.name: Computation graph view of neural networks
local_concepts_kp.description: A neural network can be understood as a directed acyclic graph of operations whose forward pass computes values and whose backward pass computes gradients.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient routing and accumulation
local_concepts_kp.description: Different node types route gradients differently: addition copies the upstream gradient, max routes it to the argmax input, multiplication swaps forward coefficients, and branches require summing contributions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Topological traversal and automatic differentiation
local_concepts_kp.description: General reverse-mode autodiff runs forward in topological order on a DAG and backward in reverse topological order, which is how modern frameworks implement backpropagation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Numeric gradient checking
local_concepts_kp.description: Central-difference finite-difference estimates provide a slow but useful way to verify that a manual backward implementation is correct.

local_concepts_kp.name: Phrase structure grammar
local_concepts_kp.description: A constituency-based view of syntax in which parts of speech combine into hierarchical phrases through rewrite rules such as noun phrase and verb phrase rules.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dependency grammar
local_concepts_kp.description: A syntactic representation built from head-dependent relations, where each word is linked to the word it modifies or depends on.

local_concepts_kp.name: Syntactic ambiguity and attachment
local_concepts_kp.description: The fact that one surface word sequence can support multiple syntactic analyses, especially through PP attachment, coordination scope, and modifier scope.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dependency trees and projectivity
local_concepts_kp.description: The structural constraints on dependency parses, including single-root trees, nesting dependencies, and the distinction between projective and nonprojective arcs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Annotated treebanks
local_concepts_kp.description: Human-annotated collections of parsed sentences used to train, compare, and evaluate dependency parsers, including Universal Dependencies.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transition-based dependency parsing
local_concepts_kp.description: A parsing strategy that builds a dependency tree through a sequence of local actions such as shift, left-arc, and right-arc over stack-buffer state.

local_concepts_kp.name: Local parser state and features
local_concepts_kp.description: The representation of a parser decision state using the stack, buffer, nearby words, parts of speech, dependency labels, and other local structural cues.

local_concepts_kp.name: UAS and LAS evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Standard parsing metrics that compare predicted dependency trees to gold trees by head correctness alone or by head-plus-label correctness.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural transition-based parser
local_concepts_kp.description: A transition parser that replaces sparse symbolic features with embeddings and a nonlinear neural classifier over parser actions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Graph-based dependency parsing
local_concepts_kp.description: A parsing approach that scores candidate arcs globally and then selects a well-formed tree, often with a tree optimization algorithm.

local_concepts_kp.name: Deep learning resurgence and scale
local_concepts_kp.description: Why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty.

local_concepts_kp.name: Modern regularization
local_concepts_kp.description: The view that very large networks can fit training data closely yet still generalize when regularization is chosen appropriately.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dropout
local_concepts_kp.description: A regularization method that randomly masks hidden activations during training and rescales at test time.

local_concepts_kp.name: Vectorization
local_concepts_kp.description: Implementing neural computation with vectors, matrices, and tensors instead of explicit loops.

local_concepts_kp.name: Parameter initialization
local_concepts_kp.description: Choosing non-constant initial weights so learning can break symmetry and remain numerically stable.

local_concepts_kp.name: Adaptive optimizers
local_concepts_kp.description: Optimization methods such as Adam that adapt updates using accumulated gradient statistics.

local_concepts_kp.name: Language modeling objective
local_concepts_kp.description: Modeling text as a probability distribution over the next token and, by chain rule, over entire sequences.

local_concepts_kp.name: N-gram language models
local_concepts_kp.description: Count-based next-word models that use a short Markov context, smoothing, and backoff.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fixed-window neural language models
local_concepts_kp.description: Neural next-word predictors that embed a limited context window and classify the next token with a softmax.

local_concepts_kp.name: Recurrent neural networks
local_concepts_kp.description: Shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context.

local_concepts_kp.name: Teacher forcing and backpropagation through time
local_concepts_kp.description: The standard training setup for autoregressive RNNs, including per-token loss, repeated-weight gradients, and truncated BPTT.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autoregressive rollout and RNN variants
local_concepts_kp.description: Generating sequences token by token and extending RNNs to character-level or conditioned-generation settings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Perplexity as language-model evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Perplexity evaluates a language model on held-out text and is the exponential of cross-entropy / average negative log-likelihood.

local_concepts_kp.name: Progress from n-gram models to neural language models
local_concepts_kp.description: Historical perplexity comparisons show why LSTMs and later models were seen as major advances over smoothed n-gram language models.

local_concepts_kp.name: Vanishing and exploding gradients in recurrent networks
local_concepts_kp.description: Repeated multiplication through time causes gradients to shrink or grow uncontrollably, limiting what simple RNNs can learn.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient clipping
local_concepts_kp.description: A practical fix for exploding gradients that rescales the gradient vector when its norm exceeds a threshold.

local_concepts_kp.name: Long-distance dependencies
local_concepts_kp.description: Language models need mechanisms that preserve learning signal across many tokens so distant context can influence predictions.

local_concepts_kp.name: LSTM cell state and hidden state
local_concepts_kp.description: LSTMs separate persistent memory in the cell state from the exposed hidden state used for ongoing prediction.

local_concepts_kp.name: LSTM gates and update equations
local_concepts_kp.description: Forget, input, and output gates regulate retention, writing, and exposure of memory through elementwise gating.

local_concepts_kp.name: Additive memory and improved gradient flow in LSTMs
local_concepts_kp.description: The additive cell path explains why LSTMs can preserve information and avoid the worst gradient-flow failures of simple RNNs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Residual and highway-style direct connections
local_concepts_kp.description: Direct and gated skip connections in deep networks are presented as a related way to preserve information and gradient flow.

local_concepts_kp.name: RNN/LSTM applications and conditional language models
local_concepts_kp.description: LSTMs can label sequences, encode whole sentences, and generate outputs conditioned on other inputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Bidirectional and stacked recurrent networks
local_concepts_kp.description: Forward-backward encoders and multi-layer recurrent stacks improve contextual representations and model capacity.

local_concepts_kp.name: Statistical machine translation
local_concepts_kp.description: Pre-neural MT relied on parallel data, Bayes-style factorization, and phrase-based models that often struggled with reordering and attachment.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural machine translation with sequence-to-sequence encoder-decoder models
local_concepts_kp.description: Neural MT uses an end-to-end encoder-decoder network that directly models target generation conditioned on a source sentence.

local_concepts_kp.name: BLEU for machine translation evaluation
local_concepts_kp.description: Automatic MT evaluation based on matching candidate n-grams against one or more reference translations, with a brevity penalty.

local_concepts_kp.name: Limitations of automatic MT metrics
local_concepts_kp.description: BLEU is useful but imperfect because translation is many-to-many and local string overlap does not fully capture meaning or grammatical role.

local_concepts_kp.name: Seq2seq bottleneck motivation for attention
local_concepts_kp.description: A single encoder hidden state is too narrow a bottleneck for long source sentences, motivating selective access to all encoder states during decoding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Core encoder-decoder attention computation
local_concepts_kp.description: At each decoding step, compute scores between the decoder state and encoder states, normalize them with softmax, form a context vector, and combine it with the decoder state to predict the next token.

local_concepts_kp.name: Attention scoring variants
local_concepts_kp.description: Attention scores can be computed with dot-product, bilinear/multiplicative, low-rank projected dot-product, or additive neural-net scoring.

local_concepts_kp.name: Benefits of attention: performance, gradients, interpretability
local_concepts_kp.description: Attention improves MT performance, alleviates the single-vector bottleneck, creates shortcut paths for gradients, and reveals alignments between source and target tokens.

local_concepts_kp.name: Default versus custom final project choice
local_concepts_kp.description: Students may either complete the guided default BERT-based project or define a custom project, with expectations calibrated by team size and scope.

local_concepts_kp.name: Value add, baselines, and evaluation design
local_concepts_kp.description: Projects are judged by what new insight, comparison, or improvement they contribute, so they need baselines, measurable evaluation, and analysis rather than just model usage.

local_concepts_kp.name: Project proposal requirements
local_concepts_kp.description: The proposal combines a critical paper review with a concrete plan covering topic, data, evaluation, and ethical considerations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Compute-aware project planning
local_concepts_kp.description: Project design should respect realistic GPU and API budgets and favor lightweight methods when full-scale training is infeasible.

local_concepts_kp.name: Families of viable project types
local_concepts_kp.description: CS224N projects can be task-driven, method-driven, interpretability-oriented, theoretical, or reimplementation-based, provided they remain substantive and evaluable.

local_concepts_kp.name: LLM-era project strategy
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern course projects often build on pretrained LLMs using PEFT, in-context learning, or API-driven experimentation, but still need a genuine research question and analysis.

local_concepts_kp.name: Video as a 4D tensor
local_concepts_kp.description: A video is a sequence of image frames with an explicit temporal dimension, represented as T x 3 x H x W or 3 x T x H x W.

local_concepts_kp.name: Clip-based training and test-time averaging
local_concepts_kp.description: Long videos are handled by training on short low-FPS, low-resolution clips and averaging sampled clip predictions at inference.

local_concepts_kp.name: Single-frame CNN baseline
local_concepts_kp.description: A simple video classifier can sample frames, run a 2D CNN independently, and average probabilities.

local_concepts_kp.name: Late fusion
local_concepts_kp.description: Late fusion extracts high-level per-frame features independently and combines them near the classifier via concatenation or pooling.

local_concepts_kp.name: Early fusion
local_concepts_kp.description: Early fusion stacks frames along channels so the first 2D convolution compares frames and collapses time.

local_concepts_kp.name: 3D CNN and slow fusion
local_concepts_kp.description: 3D CNNs use 3D convolution and pooling to gradually fuse spatial and temporal information across layers.

local_concepts_kp.name: Temporal receptive field and shift invariance
local_concepts_kp.description: 3D convolution uses local temporal filters that slide over time, enabling temporal shift invariance unlike early fusion.

local_concepts_kp.name: Sports-1M and video dataset instability
local_concepts_kp.description: Sports-1M is a fine-grained sports video dataset originally distributed as YouTube URLs, making it vulnerable to deleted or modified videos.

local_concepts_kp.name: C3D
local_concepts_kp.description: C3D is an early VGG-like 3D CNN trained on Sports-1M and widely used as a pretrained feature extractor, but it is compute-heavy.

local_concepts_kp.name: Optical flow
local_concepts_kp.description: Optical flow estimates per-pixel motion between adjacent frames as horizontal and vertical displacement fields.

local_concepts_kp.name: Two-stream networks
local_concepts_kp.description: Two-stream action networks combine an RGB appearance stream with an optical-flow motion stream and aggregate predictions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Long-term temporal modeling with RNNs/LSTMs
local_concepts_kp.description: Frame or clip features can be fed to recurrent models to capture long-range dependencies and produce video-level or frame-level outputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Recurrent convolutional networks
local_concepts_kp.description: Recurrent convolutional networks combine recurrence with spatial feature maps by replacing recurrent matrix operations with convolutions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Spatio-temporal self-attention and nonlocal blocks
local_concepts_kp.description: Nonlocal blocks extend self-attention to C x T x H x W video feature maps using query, key, and value projections.

local_concepts_kp.name: I3D inflation and weight transfer
local_concepts_kp.description: I3D converts 2D image networks to video by inflating kernels and pooling layers to 3D and reusing pretrained image weights.

local_concepts_kp.name: Video transformers and masked autoencoders
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern video models use factorized attention, token pooling, and masked-autoencoder pretraining for scalable video understanding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Temporal localization and spatio-temporal detection
local_concepts_kp.description: Beyond clip classification, video understanding can localize actions in time and detect people/actions jointly in space and time.

local_concepts_kp.name: Audio-visual source separation
local_concepts_kp.description: Visual cues can guide separation of mixed audio into sources such as speakers or instruments.

local_concepts_kp.name: Efficient video understanding
local_concepts_kp.description: Efficient methods reduce computation through better clip models, salient clip sampling, and adaptive modality selection.

local_concepts_kp.name: Egocentric multimodal video and video LLMs
local_concepts_kp.description: Emerging video understanding includes smart-glasses egocentric streams, multichannel audio, and models that connect video to LLMs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Large-Scale Distributed Training
local_concepts_kp.description: The systems view that modern deep-learning models are trained by splitting one training job across many accelerators and coordinating their computation and communication.

local_concepts_kp.name: GPU Memory Hierarchy
local_concepts_kp.description: The layered organization of HBM, L2, L1/registers, and SM-local compute that determines how efficiently data can reach GPU execution units.

local_concepts_kp.name: Tensor Cores and Mixed Precision
local_concepts_kp.description: Specialized matrix-multiply units that dominate GPU throughput and typically consume low-precision inputs while accumulating in higher precision.

local_concepts_kp.name: Cluster Topology and Bandwidth Hierarchy
local_concepts_kp.description: The server-rack-pod-cluster organization of accelerator systems, where communication gets slower as traffic crosses larger topology boundaries.

local_concepts_kp.name: Data Parallelism
local_concepts_kp.description: A baseline scaling method where each GPU processes a different minibatch shard, computes a local gradient, and synchronizes gradients across replicas.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fully Sharded Data Parallelism (FSDP)
local_concepts_kp.description: A memory-saving variant of data parallelism that shards parameters, gradients, and optimizer state across GPUs and materializes weights on demand.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hybrid Sharded Data Parallelism (HSDP)
local_concepts_kp.description: A 2D composition of FSDP within groups and ordinary data parallelism across groups, chosen to better match cluster communication hierarchy.

local_concepts_kp.name: Activation Checkpointing
local_concepts_kp.description: A memory-compute tradeoff that stores fewer intermediate activations during forward and recomputes them during backward.

local_concepts_kp.name: Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU)
local_concepts_kp.description: A system-level efficiency metric measuring how much of a device's theoretical compute is turned into useful model forward/backward work in the actual training loop.

local_concepts_kp.name: Context Parallelism
local_concepts_kp.description: A strategy for splitting one long sequence across multiple GPUs, especially useful when sequence length becomes too large for a single device.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pipeline Parallelism
local_concepts_kp.description: A layer-wise split across GPUs that introduces bubble inefficiency unless multiple microbatches are interleaved through the pipeline.

local_concepts_kp.name: Tensor Parallelism
local_concepts_kp.description: A model-dimension split that partitions weight matrices across GPUs so each device computes a block-matrix slice of the output.

local_concepts_kp.name: ND Parallelism
local_concepts_kp.description: The practical composition of several parallelism axes at once, such as TP, CP, PP, and DP, to fit frontier-scale models and clusters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Self-supervised pretraining pipeline
local_concepts_kp.description: The encoder is first trained on a pretext task with targets generated from unlabeled data, then transferred to a labeled downstream task.

local_concepts_kp.name: Representation learning under label scarcity
local_concepts_kp.description: The motivation for self-supervision is that good image representations are valuable, but manual labeling is a major bottleneck at scale.

local_concepts_kp.name: Evaluation by downstream transfer
local_concepts_kp.description: Self-supervised methods should be judged mainly by how useful their learned representations are for downstream tasks, often via linear evaluation or fine-tuning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Rotation prediction
local_concepts_kp.description: A pretext task that predicts discrete image rotations to encourage representations with visual commonsense about object orientation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Rearrangement and inpainting pretext tasks
local_concepts_kp.description: Patch-ordering and masked-reconstruction objectives supply automatic supervision from image structure and context.

local_concepts_kp.name: Colorization as cross-channel prediction
local_concepts_kp.description: Predicting missing color channels from other channels, and propagating color through videos, can learn features and induce tracking-like correspondences.

local_concepts_kp.name: Masked Autoencoders (MAE)
local_concepts_kp.description: A scalable masked-reconstruction approach using a large encoder over visible patches and a lightweight decoder over visible plus mask tokens.

local_concepts_kp.name: Contrastive learning and InfoNCE
local_concepts_kp.description: A general SSL formulation that attracts positive pairs and repels negatives, optimized with the InfoNCE objective.

local_concepts_kp.name: SimCLR and MoCo design tradeoffs
local_concepts_kp.description: SimCLR uses in-batch negatives and a projection head, while MoCo uses a momentum-updated key encoder and a queue to reduce batch-size dependence.

local_concepts_kp.name: CPC and DINO as broader SSL directions
local_concepts_kp.description: CPC extends contrastive learning to sequences, while DINO uses a student-teacher self-distillation setup related to but distinct from plain contrastive learning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scalable self-supervised representation learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Momentum-based contrastive methods such as MoCo, DINO, and DINOv2 learn transferable visual features from unlabeled images and scale through queues, EMA encoders, and larger datasets.

local_concepts_kp.name: Supervised vs unsupervised learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Supervised learning fits mappings from paired data (x, y), whereas unsupervised learning starts from x alone and seeks hidden structure, representations, or density information.

local_concepts_kp.name: Discriminative modeling p(y|x)
local_concepts_kp.description: A discriminative model predicts labels or outputs conditioned on an input and allocates probability mass across possible y values for each fixed x.

local_concepts_kp.name: Generative modeling p(x) and conditional generation p(x|y)
local_concepts_kp.description: Generative models allocate probability mass over possible data x, while conditional generative models do so within each conditioning signal y, enabling sampling and outlier rejection.

local_concepts_kp.name: Maximum likelihood estimation for density models
local_concepts_kp.description: Maximum likelihood fits probabilistic models by choosing parameters that maximize the probability or log-probability of the observed dataset under an assumed density family.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autoregressive factorization via the chain rule
local_concepts_kp.description: Autoregressive models make sequence modeling tractable by factoring p(x) into conditional next-part distributions over an ordered decomposition of x.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autoencoder bottlenecks and latent codes
local_concepts_kp.description: A bottlenecked autoencoder learns compressed latent codes useful for representation learning, but plain reconstruction alone does not make the latent space sampleable for generation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Variational autoencoder latent-variable model
local_concepts_kp.description: A VAE introduces latent variables z, a prior p(z), a decoder p_theta(x|z), and an encoder q_phi(z|x) that approximates the intractable posterior over latent codes.

local_concepts_kp.name: ELBO as the VAE training objective
local_concepts_kp.description: The evidence lower bound keeps a reconstruction term and a KL-to-prior regularizer while discarding an intractable nonnegative KL term, yielding a tractable lower bound on log p(x).

local_concepts_kp.name: Reparameterization, latent sampling, and disentangling intuition
local_concepts_kp.description: VAE training samples latent variables through a differentiable reparameterization trick, and the resulting latent spaces often support sampling, interpolation, and axis-wise variation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Generative model taxonomy
local_concepts_kp.description: Distinguishes discriminative p(y|x), unconditional generative p(x), and conditional generative p(x|y) modeling, emphasizing normalization and conditioning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Implicit density modeling
local_concepts_kp.description: Models that cannot compute p(x) explicitly but can still sample from the learned distribution.

local_concepts_kp.name: Likelihood-based explicit-density recap
local_concepts_kp.description: Autoregressive models and VAEs are reviewed as maximum-likelihood or lower-bound methods for explicit density modeling.

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN latent generator distribution
local_concepts_kp.description: Sampling z from a simple prior and mapping through G(z) induces pG, which should match pdata.

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN discriminator feedback
local_concepts_kp.description: The discriminator classifies real/fake samples and supplies gradients that train the generator.

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN minimax objective
local_concepts_kp.description: The objective min_G max_D E_x log D(x)+E_z log(1-D(G(z))) defines the adversarial game.

local_concepts_kp.name: Alternating adversarial optimization
local_concepts_kp.description: GAN training alternates gradient ascent on D and gradient descent on G over V(G,D).

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN instability diagnostics
local_concepts_kp.description: GAN value and loss curves are not reliable progress indicators because the game is non-stationary and jointly adversarial.

local_concepts_kp.name: Non-saturating generator loss
local_concepts_kp.description: Replacing the generator's original objective with -log D(G(z)) gives stronger early gradients.

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN optimal discriminator theory
local_concepts_kp.description: For fixed pG, the optimal discriminator is pdata/(pdata+pG), making pG=pdata the ideal solution under assumptions.

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN architectures and latent interpolation
local_concepts_kp.description: DC-GAN, StyleGAN, and smooth latent interpolation illustrate practical GAN design and generated image quality.

local_concepts_kp.name: GAN versus VAE tradeoffs
local_concepts_kp.description: GANs can produce crisp samples but lack encoders and are unstable, while VAEs encode x to z but often blur outputs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Diffusion denoising intuition
local_concepts_kp.description: Diffusion generates by starting from noise and repeatedly removing small amounts of noise.

local_concepts_kp.name: Rectified flow formulation
local_concepts_kp.description: Rectified flow trains a network to predict a velocity vector along a straight path between data and noise.

local_concepts_kp.name: Rectified flow sampling
local_concepts_kp.description: Sampling marches from t=1 noise to t=0 data by repeatedly applying predicted velocity updates.

local_concepts_kp.name: Conditional rectified flow
local_concepts_kp.description: Conditioning adds y as an input so generation can follow labels, text, images, or other user controls.

local_concepts_kp.name: Classifier-free guidance
local_concepts_kp.description: CFG combines unconditional and conditional predictions to strengthen adherence to conditioning at sampling time.

local_concepts_kp.name: Noise schedules
