local_concepts_kp.description: Non-uniform t sampling such as logit-normal or shifted schedules emphasizes difficult noise levels and high-resolution needs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Latent diffusion models
local_concepts_kp.description: LDMs run diffusion in a compressed latent space rather than pixel space to make high-resolution generation tractable.

local_concepts_kp.name: VAE-GAN-diffusion pipeline
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern LDM systems combine a VAE-like autoencoder, GAN-style discriminator, and diffusion model.

local_concepts_kp.name: Diffusion transformer conditioning
local_concepts_kp.description: DiTs use Transformer blocks and inject timestep and text/image conditioning via scale-shift, cross-attention, or joint attention.

local_concepts_kp.name: Text-to-image diffusion pipeline
local_concepts_kp.description: Text prompts are embedded by frozen language/text encoders and used to condition latent diffusion transformers.

local_concepts_kp.name: Text-to-video diffusion scaling
local_concepts_kp.description: Text-to-video extends latent diffusion to temporal latents, creating very long sequences and high computational cost.

local_concepts_kp.name: Diffusion distillation
local_concepts_kp.description: Distillation reduces iterative sampling steps while trying to preserve sample quality.

local_concepts_kp.name: Generalized diffusion design space
local_concepts_kp.description: Different diffusion variants choose different p(t), noisy-sample functions, and prediction targets.

local_concepts_kp.name: Diffusion mathematical perspectives
local_concepts_kp.description: Diffusion can be understood as a latent-variable model, score-function learning, or solving SDEs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Autoregressive models on discrete latents
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern autoregressive generators can model sequences of discrete latents from encoder-decoder systems.

local_concepts_kp.name: Explicit vs. implicit geometry taxonomy
local_concepts_kp.description: The organizing distinction between representations that directly enumerate or parametrize surface points and representations that define geometry through a function or field.

local_concepts_kp.name: Point clouds
local_concepts_kp.description: An unordered set of 3D points, optionally with normals, often produced directly by sensors and useful as a flexible but topology-free representation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Polygon meshes
local_concepts_kp.description: A boundary representation that adds connectivity and faces to sampled points, enabling rich geometric operations and practical graphics pipelines.

local_concepts_kp.name: Parametric curves and surfaces
local_concepts_kp.description: Representations that map a low-dimensional parameter domain such as t or (u,v) into points on a 2D or 3D shape.

local_concepts_kp.name: Implicit functions for geometry
local_concepts_kp.description: A representation in which surface points satisfy a condition like f(x,y,z)=0, enabling inside/outside reasoning and later neural field methods.

local_concepts_kp.name: Level sets and voxels
local_concepts_kp.description: Grid-based discretizations of implicit fields, with voxels storing binarized occupancy values on a 3D lattice.

local_concepts_kp.name: 3D datasets and AI+geometry task space
local_concepts_kp.description: The data and problem formulations that enabled 3D learning, including large object datasets, scanned scene data, and generative/discriminative/multimodal tasks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multi-view and voxel-based 3D deep learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Early neural approaches that either render 3D shapes into multiple images for 2D CNNs or directly process voxel grids with 3D convolution.

local_concepts_kp.name: PointNet and point-set invariances
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that point-cloud networks should respect permutation and sampling invariance, implemented by symmetric aggregation over point features.

local_concepts_kp.name: AtlasNet and deep implicit shape decoders
local_concepts_kp.description: Learned mappings from low-dimensional parameter spaces or x,y,z queries to geometry, covering both patch-based surface decoders and occupancy/SDF-style networks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural radiance fields and differentiable volume rendering
local_concepts_kp.description: A neural field that predicts density and color from spatial position and view direction, trained through differentiable rendering from 2D images.

local_concepts_kp.name: 3D Gaussian splatting
local_concepts_kp.description: A sparse radiance-field-style representation that stores extended Gaussian blobs in space to avoid repeatedly sampling empty regions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Structure-aware and programmatic shape representations
local_concepts_kp.description: Representations that encode parts, relations, hierarchies, or executable programs so models can capture symmetry, repetition, and semantics beyond raw geometry.

local_concepts_kp.name: Foundation-model pretraining and adaptation paradigm
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea of pretraining one broad model on diverse skills and adapting it to many downstream tasks with fine-tuning, few-shot, or zero-shot use.

local_concepts_kp.name: Traits of foundation models
local_concepts_kp.description: Foundation models are typically general and robust across tasks, and often use large parameter counts, large datasets, and self-supervised objectives.

local_concepts_kp.name: SimCLR contrastive representation learning
local_concepts_kp.description: A self-supervised objective that pulls together augmented views of the same image and pushes apart different images.

local_concepts_kp.name: CLIP symmetric image-text contrastive objective
local_concepts_kp.description: CLIP trains an image encoder and text encoder so matching image-text pairs are close and mismatched pairs are far in a shared embedding space.

local_concepts_kp.name: Internet-scale image-text data
local_concepts_kp.description: Web image-text associations provide scalable supervision for CLIP-style multimodal pretraining.

local_concepts_kp.name: Linear probing and downstream transfer
local_concepts_kp.description: A pre-trained encoder can be reused for downstream tasks by adding lightweight supervised heads such as linear classifiers or decoders.

local_concepts_kp.name: Zero-shot CLIP classification as nearest neighbor over text prototypes
local_concepts_kp.description: CLIP can classify new images by embedding candidate class labels with the text encoder and choosing the most similar text embedding.

local_concepts_kp.name: Prompt templates and prompt ensembling for CLIP
local_concepts_kp.description: Using phrases and averaging multiple prompt embeddings improves class representations compared with bare class names.

local_concepts_kp.name: CLIP out-of-distribution robustness
local_concepts_kp.description: CLIP can generalize to unusual datasets such as ObjectNet because language supervision and data scale expose richer visual structure than category labels alone.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scaling model size and training data
local_concepts_kp.description: CLIP performance is attributed partly to scaling up model parameters and image-text training data.

local_concepts_kp.name: CoCa contrastive captioning objective
local_concepts_kp.description: CoCa augments CLIP-style contrastive learning with a decoder and captioning loss to learn richer visual representations.

local_concepts_kp.name: CLIP retrieval, open-vocabulary use, and chainability
local_concepts_kp.description: CLIP is useful because its dot-product similarity enables fast retrieval, arbitrary text queries, and composition with other models.

local_concepts_kp.name: CLIP limitations: batch size, compositionality, grounding, and coverage
local_concepts_kp.description: CLIP struggles with relational compositionality, depends on large/hard-negative batches, lacks localization grounding, and cannot cover all concepts through web data alone.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodal autoregressive language-model framing
local_concepts_kp.description: Vision-language models condition an autoregressive language model on image tokens so visual tasks can be handled through text generation.

local_concepts_kp.name: LLaVA CLIP-to-LLM connector architecture
local_concepts_kp.description: LLaVA uses CLIP visual features, typically penultimate patch tokens, and maps them through a trainable linear connector into an LLM.

local_concepts_kp.name: Flamingo perceiver sampler and gated cross-attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Flamingo fuses vision into an LLM by sampling visual tokens and injecting them into every layer through gated cross-attention modules.

local_concepts_kp.name: Interleaved image-text training and masking
local_concepts_kp.description: Flamingo trains on long interleaved image-text sequences while masking attention so each description attends to the correct image.

local_concepts_kp.name: In-context and few-shot multimodal reasoning
local_concepts_kp.description: A multimodal language model can infer new visual task behavior from example image-text or image-QA demonstrations in the prompt.

local_concepts_kp.name: Benchmark shift to broad visual question answering
local_concepts_kp.description: As multimodal language models improved, evaluation broadened from image classification to many visual understanding tasks framed as QA.

local_concepts_kp.name: Closed-model distillation and open reproducibility gap
local_concepts_kp.description: Many strong open VLMs were distilled from closed models, leaving the community without a fully reproducible training recipe.

local_concepts_kp.name: Molmo open multimodal language model
local_concepts_kp.description: Molmo is presented as an open-weights, open-data, open-code VLM that competitively matches closed models in user preference evaluations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Grounded decision-making through pointing
local_concepts_kp.description: A VLM can reduce hallucination and improve interpretability by producing visual points tied to the evidence used for an answer.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dense curated visual descriptions
local_concepts_kp.description: High-quality curated annotations capture spatial, positional, material, and size information that incidental internet captions omit.

local_concepts_kp.name: Chaining grounded VLM outputs into other models
local_concepts_kp.description: Grounded outputs such as points can become prompts or control signals for models like SAM or robotics planners.

local_concepts_kp.name: SAM promptable segmentation architecture
local_concepts_kp.description: SAM combines an image encoder, prompt encoder, and lightweight mask decoder to produce masks from text, points, boxes, or other prompts.

local_concepts_kp.name: SAM ambiguity handling and data engine
local_concepts_kp.description: SAM handles ambiguous prompts by outputting multiple masks and relies on massive human/model-in-the-loop mask collection.

local_concepts_kp.name: Chaining and visual programming
local_concepts_kp.description: Systems such as VisProg use LLM-generated programs to call specialized vision models and combine their outputs for complex visual tasks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Verification, distillation, and tool-building frontiers
local_concepts_kp.description: Current research tries to verify model outputs, distill chained capabilities into single models, and build new tools automatically when needed.

local_concepts_kp.name: Robot learning as interactive sequential decision-making
local_concepts_kp.description: Robot learning is distinguished from standard prediction problems because actions change the environment, generate new observations, and must be chosen over time to improve reward.

local_concepts_kp.name: Goal-state-action-reward formulation
local_concepts_kp.description: Robot learning problems are structured around a task goal, current state, action choice, reward signal, and resulting next state.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reward design and task specification
local_concepts_kp.description: Reward functions are not uniquely determined by the task name; they encode the specific preferences and metrics that define success in an application.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multimodal robot perception
local_concepts_kp.description: Robots need to fuse multiple sensory streams such as vision, depth, touch, and audio to recover task-relevant structure from partial, noisy observations.

local_concepts_kp.name: Embodied, active, and situated robot vision
local_concepts_kp.description: Robot vision differs from passive computer vision because the robot has a body, can choose viewpoints or perturbations, and operates in the here-and-now of a specific physical context.

local_concepts_kp.name: Perception-action loop and task-focused sensing
local_concepts_kp.description: Robots should sense only what is necessary for the current task and continuously couple perception to action rather than fully reconstructing the world at all times.

local_concepts_kp.name: Reinforcement learning through trial and error
local_concepts_kp.description: Reinforcement learning trains an agent by repeatedly interacting with an environment and shifting behavior toward actions that produce higher return.

local_concepts_kp.name: Why RL is harder than supervised learning
local_concepts_kp.description: RL must cope with stochastic dynamics, delayed credit assignment, non-differentiable environments, and nonstationarity caused by the agent’s own actions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Q-functions and value-based action selection
local_concepts_kp.description: A Q-function predicts expected discounted future reward for a state-action pair and supports greedy action selection by choosing the highest-value action.

local_concepts_kp.name: Model-free RL successes and sim-to-real limitations
local_concepts_kp.description: RL has produced dramatic wins in games and locomotion, yet still suffers from sample inefficiency, safety concerns, and limited robustness in many manipulation settings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Learned forward dynamics for model-based planning
local_concepts_kp.description: Instead of directly learning a policy, a robot can learn how actions change the world and use that model to optimize future action sequences.

local_concepts_kp.name: State representations for learned world models
local_concepts_kp.description: World models can represent state at different granularities—pixels, keypoints, or particles—and the right choice depends on the task and object structure.

local_concepts_kp.name: Behavior cloning and iterative correction
local_concepts_kp.description: A policy can be supervised on demonstrations, but practical imitation learning usually needs repeated expert correction to counter distribution shift and cascading error.

local_concepts_kp.name: Inverse RL, implicit BC, and diffusion policies
local_concepts_kp.description: More expressive imitation-learning methods infer rewards or use generative-policy parameterizations to better capture multimodal demonstrations and rich manipulation behavior.

local_concepts_kp.name: Robotic foundation models with pretraining and post-training
local_concepts_kp.description: Robotic foundation models aim for broad goal-conditioned action generalization by combining large aggregated robot datasets with pretrained VLMs and task-specific post-training.

local_concepts_kp.name: Evaluation bottlenecks and foundation world models
local_concepts_kp.description: Robot learning progress is hard to measure because real-world evaluation is expensive and simulation is imperfect, motivating better benchmarks and foundation world models trained from action-conditioned data.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human-centered AI framing
local_concepts_kp.description: The lecture frames AI progress through the lens of human perception, human impact, and human values rather than only algorithmic performance.

local_concepts_kp.name: Object recognition as a foundation of visual intelligence
local_concepts_kp.description: Object recognition is treated as a core capability of both human and machine vision and as the starting point of modern computer vision progress.

local_concepts_kp.name: Data-driven statistical learning for recognition
local_concepts_kp.description: Pre-deep-learning progress came from shifting from hand-coded rules to learnable statistical models trained from data.

local_concepts_kp.name: ImageNet-era convergence of data, compute, and neural networks
local_concepts_kp.description: Large-scale datasets, GPU computation, and neural architectures converged to unlock modern deep learning for vision.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scene graphs and relational perception
local_concepts_kp.description: Scene understanding requires explicit representations of objects, attributes, and relations rather than isolated labels.

local_concepts_kp.name: Image captioning and multimodal scene description
local_concepts_kp.description: Vision systems can map images to natural language descriptions, first with CNN+LSTM pipelines and later with multimodal LLMs.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dynamic multi-actor activity understanding
local_concepts_kp.description: Understanding who is doing what and what happens next in videos remains an open problem beyond static scene recognition.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fine-grained recognition for superhuman perception
local_concepts_kp.description: AI can exceed typical human perception by distinguishing extremely similar categories and extracting patterns at scale.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human attention limits and error-prone perception
local_concepts_kp.description: Human visual processing has systematic limits such as Stroop interference and change blindness that can create safety risks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Bias and fairness in visual AI
local_concepts_kp.description: AI systems can inherit and amplify human or data bias, making fairness analysis essential for deployment.

local_concepts_kp.name: Privacy-preserving visual AI
local_concepts_kp.description: Vision systems should preserve useful activity signals while withholding identity or other sensitive information.

local_concepts_kp.name: AI augmentation in healthcare
local_concepts_kp.description: Smart sensing and machine learning can compensate for labor shortages by supporting monitoring and decision-making in clinical and home settings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Embodied AI as perception-action integration
local_concepts_kp.description: Robotics is framed as the closure of the loop between seeing and doing, requiring both perception and action planning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human-preference-driven robot benchmarking
local_concepts_kp.description: Robot benchmarks should prioritize tasks people actually want help with and should be evaluated in realistic, diverse environments.

local_concepts_kp.name: Augmentation over replacement
local_concepts_kp.description: The lecture's closing principle is that AI should enhance human capability and care rather than simply replace people.

local_concepts_kp.name: Visual intelligence as a cornerstone of intelligence
local_concepts_kp.description: The lecture frames vision as central to animal and human intelligence, not merely one application area inside AI.

local_concepts_kp.name: CS231n focuses on the intersection of deep learning and computer vision
local_concepts_kp.description: The class is explicitly scoped to the overlap between deep learning methods and core computer vision problems.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hierarchical receptive fields in biological vision
local_concepts_kp.description: Hubel and Wiesel's findings that local receptive fields and hierarchical composition underlie early visual processing.

local_concepts_kp.name: 3D vision is fundamentally ill-posed from 2D images
local_concepts_kp.description: Inferring full 3D structure from 2D projections is mathematically hard and motivates multiple views, correspondence, and geometric reasoning.

local_concepts_kp.name: Classical computer vision before deep learning
local_concepts_kp.description: The pre-deep-learning era emphasized parts, edges, grouping, matching, and handcrafted representations, with limited success on full recognition.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human recognition is fast, contextual, and specialized
local_concepts_kp.description: Cognitive and neuroscience studies show that recognition depends on scene context, happens rapidly, and recruits specialized processing areas.

local_concepts_kp.name: Deep learning resurgence was driven by data, algorithms, and compute
local_concepts_kp.description: Modern computer vision took off when scalable data, better learning algorithms, and powerful hardware converged.

local_concepts_kp.name: Modern vision extends beyond classification
local_concepts_kp.description: The course treats vision as covering segmentation, detection, video, multimodal learning, generative models, vision-language models, 3D vision, and embodied intelligence.

local_concepts_kp.name: Human-centered implications of computer vision
local_concepts_kp.description: Vision systems can save lives and create value, but also propagate bias and cause harm in high-stakes settings.

local_concepts_kp.name: CS231n curriculum structure and learning objectives
local_concepts_kp.description: The course is organized around deep learning basics, visual understanding, generative/interactive intelligence, and human-centered AI, with explicit goals for problem formulation, model building, and field awareness.

local_concepts_kp.name: Image classification task
local_concepts_kp.description: The task of assigning one label from a fixed vocabulary to an input image.

local_concepts_kp.name: Semantic gap and visual nuisance factors
local_concepts_kp.description: The mismatch between human object understanding and machine access to raw pixels, compounded by viewpoint, illumination, clutter, occlusion, deformation, intraclass variation, and context.

local_concepts_kp.name: Data-driven supervised learning pipeline
local_concepts_kp.description: The collect-train-predict paradigm: gather labeled data, fit a model, and evaluate predictions on unseen examples.

local_concepts_kp.name: Nearest neighbor classification
local_concepts_kp.description: A nonparametric classifier that stores all labeled examples and predicts by copying the label of the closest stored example.

local_concepts_kp.name: Distance metrics for similarity
local_concepts_kp.description: The choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hyperparameter tuning with validation and cross-validation
local_concepts_kp.description: Model settings such as K and distance metric must be selected using held-out validation procedures rather than training or test data.

local_concepts_kp.name: Failure of pixel-space nearest neighbors
local_concepts_kp.description: Raw pixel distance is not semantically meaningful for images, so visually wrong neighbors can appear close and tiny transformations can break similarity.

local_concepts_kp.name: Linear classifier as a parametric score function
local_concepts_kp.description: A learned affine map f(x,W)=Wx+b that converts a flattened image into class scores.

local_concepts_kp.name: Visual and geometric interpretation of weights and bias
local_concepts_kp.description: Rows of W can be viewed as class templates, while geometrically the affine map induces class-separating hyperplanes that are shifted by the bias term.

local_concepts_kp.name: Limits of linear separability and the need for an objective
local_concepts_kp.description: Some label layouts cannot be separated by a single hyperplane, so learning requires a loss function that scores how well the current parameters behave on training data.

local_concepts_kp.name: Softmax probabilities and cross-entropy loss
local_concepts_kp.description: Transform raw class scores into probabilities with softmax and learn by minimizing the negative log probability of the correct class, equivalent here to maximum likelihood and cross-entropy under one-hot labels.

local_concepts_kp.name: Image classification and recognition challenges
local_concepts_kp.description: Assigning an image to a label despite semantic gap, viewpoint, illumination, deformation, occlusion, clutter, and intraclass variation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Data-driven kNN and validation/test splits
local_concepts_kp.description: Using nearest training examples to classify and using validation data to select hyperparameters while preserving test data for final evaluation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Linear classifier scores and loss functions
local_concepts_kp.description: Linear score functions, Softmax/cross-entropy, and dataset loss as an average of per-example losses.

local_concepts_kp.name: Regularization and generalization
local_concepts_kp.description: Adding a penalty to reduce overfitting and improve unseen-data performance.

local_concepts_kp.name: L1, L2, elastic net, and lambda
local_concepts_kp.description: Common regularization penalties and the lambda hyperparameter controlling penalty strength.

local_concepts_kp.name: Weight-preference effects of regularization
local_concepts_kp.description: Regularizers encode preferences such as L2 spread-out weights and L1 sparsity.

local_concepts_kp.name: Loss landscapes and optimization objective
local_concepts_kp.description: Viewing loss as a landscape over parameters, where training searches for low-loss settings.

local_concepts_kp.name: Numerical gradients, analytic gradients, and gradient checking
local_concepts_kp.description: Finite-difference derivative approximations, exact calculus-based gradients, and implementation checks comparing the two.

local_concepts_kp.name: Gradient descent and stochastic gradient descent
local_concepts_kp.description: Iteratively updating weights by negative gradients, using minibatches for scalable stochastic estimates.

local_concepts_kp.name: SGD failure modes
local_concepts_kp.description: Poor conditioning, overshoot, local minima, saddle points, and minibatch gradient noise.

local_concepts_kp.name: Momentum optimizer
local_concepts_kp.description: Using velocity or a running average of gradients to smooth updates and keep moving in useful directions.

local_concepts_kp.name: RMSProp and adaptive learning rates
local_concepts_kp.description: Element-wise update scaling by historical squared gradients, giving per-parameter adaptive learning rates.

local_concepts_kp.name: Adam and AdamW
local_concepts_kp.description: Adam combines momentum and RMSProp-style adaptivity; AdamW decouples weight decay from moment estimation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Learning-rate schedules, warmup, and batch-size scaling
local_concepts_kp.description: Changing learning rates during training using step, cosine, linear, inverse-square-root schedules, warmup, and linear scaling with batch size.

local_concepts_kp.name: Second-order optimization and Hessian limitations
local_concepts_kp.description: Using Hessians and quadratic approximations, and why these methods are usually impractical for large deep networks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Neural-network nonlinearity preview
local_concepts_kp.description: A two-layer neural network adds a nonlinearity between weight matrices to transform data into a linearly separable representation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Loss functions and gradient-based optimization refresher
local_concepts_kp.description: The prior-lecture setup of score functions, losses, regularization, gradients, and optimization algorithms that neural-network training builds on.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fully connected neural networks / MLPs
local_concepts_kp.description: Stacks of linear layers that map inputs to outputs through hidden representations, more precisely called fully connected networks or multi-layer perceptrons.

local_concepts_kp.name: Nonlinearity as the source of neural-network expressive power
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that activations between linear maps prevent the network from collapsing into a single linear transformation and allow nonlinear decision boundaries.

local_concepts_kp.name: Activation-function trade-offs
local_concepts_kp.description: Practical differences among ReLU, leaky ReLU, ELU, GELU, SiLU, sigmoid, and tanh, especially around dead neurons and vanishing gradients.

local_concepts_kp.name: Forward pass and hand-built 2-layer training loop
local_concepts_kp.description: The minimal implementation pattern for a small neural network: define dimensions and weights, run the forward pass, compute loss, backpropagate, and update parameters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Model capacity versus regularization
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that more neurons increase expressive power and overfitting risk, so regularization—not shrinking the architecture by default—should control generalization.

local_concepts_kp.name: Computational graphs
local_concepts_kp.description: A graph representation of operations and intermediates from inputs and parameters to the scalar loss, enabling modular gradient computation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Backpropagation as recursive chain rule
local_concepts_kp.description: The backward traversal of a computational graph that repeatedly applies the chain rule to obtain gradients for all relevant variables and parameters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Local, upstream, and downstream gradients
local_concepts_kp.description: The node-level view of backprop where each node multiplies its local derivative by an incoming upstream gradient to produce gradients for earlier nodes.

local_concepts_kp.name: Modular forward/backward operators
local_concepts_kp.description: The implementation pattern where each operation exposes forward() and backward() behavior and stores intermediates needed for gradient computation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Vector-valued gradients and Jacobians
local_concepts_kp.description: The extension from scalar derivatives to vector-to-scalar and vector-to-vector settings, where Jacobians encode how each output component depends on each input component.

local_concepts_kp.name: Efficient matrix backprop without explicit Jacobians
local_concepts_kp.description: Using structured matrix formulas for backward passes so large tensor operations remain tractable without materializing massive Jacobian matrices.

local_concepts_kp.name: Tensorized image classification pipeline
local_concepts_kp.description: Formulating image classification as a mapping from image tensors to category-score tensors, then fitting the mapping with a model, loss, and optimizer.

local_concepts_kp.name: Loss-driven optimization
local_concepts_kp.description: Using classification losses to score parameter settings and optimization algorithms such as SGD or Adam to search for low-loss solutions.

local_concepts_kp.name: Limits of linear classifiers
local_concepts_kp.description: Linear classifiers are weak for vision because they compress each class into a single template and can only create linear decision boundaries.

local_concepts_kp.name: Computational graphs and backpropagation
local_concepts_kp.description: Viewing networks as graphs of local operators so gradients can be propagated backwards using only local derivatives and the chain rule.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hand-crafted feature representations
local_concepts_kp.description: Pre-neural-network image pipelines that replaced raw pixels with human-designed descriptors such as color histograms or HOG before classification.

local_concepts_kp.name: End-to-end learning for images
local_concepts_kp.description: Replacing hand-engineered feature stages with trainable image-processing operators that preserve spatial structure and are learned from data.

local_concepts_kp.name: CNN architecture pattern and historical role
local_concepts_kp.description: CNNs pair convolutional feature extraction with classifier heads and were the dominant vision architecture from LeNet through the AlexNet era before transformers became more common.

local_concepts_kp.name: Convolution as local template matching
local_concepts_kp.description: A convolution layer preserves spatial structure by sliding small filters over local image patches and turning filter responses into activation maps.

local_concepts_kp.name: Convolution hyperparameters and tensor shapes
local_concepts_kp.description: Understanding filter count, kernel size, stride, padding, and the resulting input/output tensor shapes for convolution layers.

local_concepts_kp.name: Receptive fields and strided downsampling
local_concepts_kp.description: The receptive field measures how much of the original image can affect a later activation; stride and downsampling let that field grow faster with depth.

local_concepts_kp.name: Pooling and translation equivariance
local_concepts_kp.description: Pooling offers cheap spatial downsampling, and together with convolution exhibits translation equivariance, a property that matches how image content should be processed.

local_concepts_kp.name: CNN layer composition
local_concepts_kp.description: The idea that a CNN is built by composing convolutions, pooling, fully connected layers, normalization, activations, and sometimes dropout into a trainable architecture.

local_concepts_kp.name: Normalization layers
local_concepts_kp.description: Methods that normalize activations and then apply learned scale and shift, differing mainly in how they compute mean and standard deviation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dropout regularization
local_concepts_kp.description: A train-time masking technique that randomly zeros activations so the network cannot rely on brittle co-adapted features.

local_concepts_kp.name: Activation function choice
local_concepts_kp.description: The selection of nonlinearities based on gradient behavior, smoothness, and computational cost, with ReLU and GELU as the main practical choices.

local_concepts_kp.name: Receptive field and small-filter design
local_concepts_kp.description: The design principle that stacking small convolutions can match the receptive field of larger filters while using fewer parameters and adding more nonlinearities.

local_concepts_kp.name: Residual connections
local_concepts_kp.description: Skip connections that add x back to F(x), making identity mappings easy and enabling optimization of much deeper networks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Kaiming/MSRA initialization
local_concepts_kp.description: A ReLU-aware initialization rule that scales weights by sqrt(2 / D_in) to keep activation statistics stable through depth.

local_concepts_kp.name: Image normalization preprocessing
local_concepts_kp.description: Per-channel centering and scaling of image inputs before training or inference.

local_concepts_kp.name: Data augmentation
local_concepts_kp.description: Label-preserving input transformations used during training to improve generalization and reduce memorization.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transfer learning
local_concepts_kp.description: Reusing pretrained CNN features or pretrained weights to solve a new task more effectively when target data is limited.

local_concepts_kp.name: Hyperparameter debugging and search
local_concepts_kp.description: A practical workflow for validating a training setup, choosing learning rates, reading train/validation curves, and searching hyperparameters efficiently.

local_concepts_kp.name: Dropout and normalization implementation caveats
local_concepts_kp.description: Practical details for dropout test-time scaling and when normalization helps or hurts.

local_concepts_kp.name: Sequence modeling regimes
local_concepts_kp.description: One-to-many, many-to-one, and many-to-many formulations for variable-length input/output tasks.

local_concepts_kp.name: RNN hidden-state recurrence
local_concepts_kp.description: Updating a hidden state from current input and previous hidden state at every time step.

local_concepts_kp.name: Vanilla RNN equations
local_concepts_kp.description: The tanh-based recurrent update and output projection used as the baseline RNN formulation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Manual RNN state construction
local_concepts_kp.description: Designing hidden-state contents and weights by reasoning from a toy sequence task.

local_concepts_kp.name: Shared-weight computational graph
local_concepts_kp.description: Unrolling an RNN while reusing the same parameters at every time step.

local_concepts_kp.name: Backpropagation through time
local_concepts_kp.description: Computing gradients through the unrolled RNN and summing contributions to shared weights.

local_concepts_kp.name: Truncated BPTT
local_concepts_kp.description: A fixed-window training approximation that passes hidden states forward but cuts gradient history.

local_concepts_kp.name: Character-level autoregressive language modeling
local_concepts_kp.description: Predicting the next character/token, feeding generated outputs back in, and sampling from probabilities.

local_concepts_kp.name: RNN tradeoffs and applications
local_concepts_kp.description: Arbitrary-length processing and weight sharing versus sequential computation and fixed-state bottlenecks, with CV applications.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multilayer RNNs
local_concepts_kp.description: Stacking recurrent layers over depth while sharing weights over time within each layer.

local_concepts_kp.name: Vanishing and exploding gradients
local_concepts_kp.description: Gradient-flow pathologies from repeated nonlinear and recurrent matrix multiplications.

local_concepts_kp.name: LSTM gates and cell state
local_concepts_kp.description: Gated recurrent architecture that selectively writes, forgets, outputs, and preserves information.

local_concepts_kp.name: Modern RNN-like state-space models
local_concepts_kp.description: RWKV, Mamba, and related models that revive RNN-like linear-time sequence modeling.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fixed-length context bottleneck in seq2seq RNNs
local_concepts_kp.description: Encoder-decoder RNNs compress the input sequence into one context vector, which limits performance on longer sequences because all input-output communication must pass through that fixed-size representation.

local_concepts_kp.name: Alignment scores and attention weights
local_concepts_kp.description: Attention begins by comparing a decoder/query state with each candidate source state, then applying softmax to turn those scores into normalized attention weights.

local_concepts_kp.name: Context vector as a weighted sum
local_concepts_kp.description: The output of attention is a context vector formed by weighting source hidden states with attention weights and summing them.

local_concepts_kp.name: Attention heatmaps as learned alignments
local_concepts_kp.description: Visualizing attention matrices can reveal approximate word alignments, reorderings, and partial many-to-many correspondences learned without explicit alignment supervision.

local_concepts_kp.name: Attention as a query-to-data operator
local_concepts_kp.description: Abstractly, attention maps query vectors and data vectors to output vectors by letting each query retrieve a custom summary of the data.

local_concepts_kp.name: Scaled dot-product attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Using a dot product as similarity and scaling it by the square root of the feature dimension stabilizes softmax behavior and gradient flow.

local_concepts_kp.name: Keys and values
local_concepts_kp.description: Attention separates matching and retrieval by projecting data vectors into keys used for similarity computation and values used for the weighted output.

local_concepts_kp.name: Cross-attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Cross-attention uses one set of query vectors to retrieve information from a separate set of data vectors, often from another modality or sequence.

local_concepts_kp.name: Self-attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Self-attention applies attention within one set of vectors by projecting each input into a query, key, and value so every element can mix information from the whole set.

local_concepts_kp.name: Permutation equivariance and positional embeddings
local_concepts_kp.description: Because self-attention on its own does not care about order beyond corresponding shuffles, positional embeddings are added when order information matters.

local_concepts_kp.name: Masked self-attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Masking blocks selected attention links by forcing their pre-softmax scores to negative infinity, which is essential for autoregressive language modeling.

local_concepts_kp.name: Multi-head self-attention
local_concepts_kp.description: Multi-head attention runs several independent attention heads in parallel and fuses them, increasing capacity while staying hardware-friendly.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transformer block architecture
local_concepts_kp.description: A transformer block combines self-attention, residual connections, normalization, and an MLP/FFN, and a full transformer is a stack of these blocks.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transformers as a cross-domain backbone
local_concepts_kp.description: The same transformer architecture supports next-token language models and vision transformers over image patches, and the lecture frames it as the dominant backbone for large AI models.

local_concepts_kp.name: ViT patch tokenization and positional encoding
local_concepts_kp.description: Representing an image as a sequence of patch tokens, then restoring spatial order with positional embeddings before transformer processing.

local_concepts_kp.name: ViT readout strategies for classification
local_concepts_kp.description: Two common ways to turn transformer outputs into image classes: a learned class token or pooling all output tokens before classification.

local_concepts_kp.name: Transformer stability and capacity tweaks
local_concepts_kp.description: Pre-norm, RMSNorm, SwiGLU, and mixture-of-experts are practical modifications that stabilize training or increase model capacity.

local_concepts_kp.name: Semantic segmentation as per-pixel prediction
local_concepts_kp.description: Semantic segmentation labels each pixel with a class label, turning structured scene understanding into dense classification.

local_concepts_kp.name: Fully convolutional segmentation and per-pixel loss
local_concepts_kp.description: FCN-style segmentation predicts an entire label map with convolutional encoder-decoder structure and is trained by aggregating classification losses across pixels.

local_concepts_kp.name: Upsampling and U-Net skip connections
local_concepts_kp.description: Decoder-side upsampling can use unpooling or transposed convolution, and U-Net improves dense prediction by copying encoder features into the decoder.

local_concepts_kp.name: Object detection as joint classification and localization
local_concepts_kp.description: Detection extends classification by predicting both an object category and bounding-box coordinates for each object.

local_concepts_kp.name: Region proposals and the R-CNN progression
local_concepts_kp.description: R-CNN, Fast R-CNN, and Faster R-CNN progressively reduce detection cost by sharing features and learning proposal generation.

local_concepts_kp.name: YOLO one-stage detection
local_concepts_kp.description: YOLO predicts boxes and classes for all grid cells in a single pass and then filters them with confidence-based postprocessing.

local_concepts_kp.name: DETR object queries
local_concepts_kp.description: DETR uses learned decoder queries to emit a set of object predictions, each containing a class, a box, or a no-object output.

local_concepts_kp.name: Mask R-CNN for instance segmentation
local_concepts_kp.description: Mask R-CNN augments a detection pipeline with a per-instance mask head so each detected object also receives a pixel mask.

local_concepts_kp.name: Saliency via input gradients
local_concepts_kp.description: Saliency maps identify input pixels that most affect a chosen class score by differentiating the score with respect to the input image.

local_concepts_kp.name: CAM and Grad-CAM
local_concepts_kp.description: CAM localizes class evidence with weighted feature maps, while Grad-CAM generalizes the idea by deriving weights from gradients at a chosen layer.

local_concepts_kp.name: ViT attention visualization
local_concepts_kp.description: Transformer attention weights can be visualized directly over image patches, giving a more native explanation route than CNN-specific activation methods.
